skill.name: whatsapp-automation
skill.description: Automate WhatsApp Business workflows through Rube MCP, including messages, templates, media, contacts, and business profile lookups.
skill.summary: Automate WhatsApp Business messaging and template workflows through Rube MCP.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: The static command execution, weak cryptography, and system reconnaissance alerts are false positives caused by Markdown backticks, tool names, and WhatsApp API terminology. The confirmed risk is networked operation through Rube MCP and WhatsApp Business APIs, which can expose message content, contact details, media, and business metadata to external services. No prompt injection text, malicious intent, credential theft, or local code execution was found in SKILL.md.

medium_findings.title: Third-Party Network Service Handles WhatsApp Data
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The skill instructs users to configure the Rube MCP endpoint and use it for WhatsApp Business operations. Message bodies, contact records, media references, templates, and business profile data may pass through external services. This is legitimate for the skill purpose, but it creates privacy and compliance risk.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file explicitly requires Rube MCP and documents WhatsApp message, media, and contact operations. The usage is expected, but the data flow to external network services is clear.

medium_findings.title: Outbound Messaging Can Trigger Policy, Cost, and Abuse Risks
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The skill enables outbound WhatsApp messages and templates. It documents the 24-hour customer window, template requirements, and per-conversation costs, which confirms the workflow can affect recipients and billing if misused.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The risk follows directly from documented sending workflows and the stated WhatsApp restrictions. It is not malicious, but it requires user authorization and operational safeguards.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Tool Names Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The repeated backtick detections are Markdown formatting around Rube and WhatsApp tool names, parameters, and fenced workflow examples. SKILL.md contains guidance text only and no Ruby code, shell scripts, or executable command construction.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Inspection confirms these lines are Markdown inline code or numbered examples. There is no executable Ruby backtick syntax or shell invocation context.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Alert Has No Crypto Context
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The weak cryptography matches do not correspond to hashing or encryption calls. The referenced lines contain the skill description and contact phone number guidance, not cryptographic algorithm usage.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is documentation and contains no code path that selects or applies a cryptographic algorithm. The line contents are unrelated to cryptography.

low_findings.title: False Positive: WhatsApp API Lookups Flagged as System Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The referenced lines describe WhatsApp message IDs, phone number IDs, media IDs, templates, and business profile API calls. They do not query the local operating system or collect host information.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context is remote WhatsApp Business resource lookup. No local host reconnaissance commands, files, or environment probes are present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Hardcoded URL Is Setup Documentation
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The hardcoded URL points to the documented Rube MCP server that users must configure for this integration. It is not an unexpected callback, hidden exfiltration endpoint, or obfuscated destination.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL appears in the setup section and matches the declared dependency on Rube MCP. Network use remains a real operational risk, but the URL itself is not malicious evidence.

content.value_statement: Teams need reliable help for WhatsApp Business workflows without memorizing every API field. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through Rube MCP tool discovery, message sending, templates, media, contacts, and profile tasks.
actual_capabilities: Guides tool discovery before each WhatsApp Business workflow.
Sends text messages, replies, approved templates, media, and contact cards.
Explains phone number ID lookup before send operations.
Documents template creation, status checks, categories, and language requirements.
Covers WhatsApp 24-hour messaging windows and common delivery pitfalls.
Supports business profile and registered phone number lookup tasks.
limitations: Requires an active Rube MCP connection and WhatsApp Business account.
Does not work with regular personal WhatsApp accounts.
Template messages require Meta approval before they can be sent.
Media uploads and public media URLs must follow WhatsApp size and format limits.

use_cases.title: Send Customer Support Replies
use_cases.description: Reply to recent customer WhatsApp messages with the correct phone number ID, message ID, and body fields.

use_cases.title: Run Approved Notification Campaigns
use_cases.description: Find approved templates, verify language codes, and send utility or marketing messages within WhatsApp policy limits.

use_cases.title: Share Documents and Contacts
use_cases.description: Upload media, send documents by media ID or URL, and send structured contact cards to customers.

prompt_templates.title: Send a Basic Message
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the WhatsApp automation skill to send a text message. First search the current Rube tool schema, then list required fields before sending.

prompt_templates.title: Check and Send a Template
prompt_templates.prompt: Find my approved WhatsApp templates, confirm the status and language code, then help me send the right template to this recipient.

prompt_templates.title: Send Media Safely
prompt_templates.prompt: Prepare a WhatsApp media message. Check whether upload or public URL sending is better, then identify required fields and limits.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Full Messaging Flow
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan a WhatsApp Business workflow for support follow-ups, including phone number lookup, 24-hour window rules, templates, replies, and failure checks.

output_examples.input: Send a delivery update to a customer after the 24-hour window has closed.
output: The assistant checks available templates before sending.
It selects an approved utility template and confirms the language code.
It asks for missing recipient and template variable values before action.

output_examples.input: Send a product brochure PDF to a WhatsApp contact.
output: The assistant compares upload and public URL options.
It confirms document size, recipient format, and business phone number ID.
It sends the media only after required fields are complete.

output_examples.input: Create a new appointment reminder template.
output: The assistant identifies template name, category, language, and variable requirements.
It notes that Meta approval is required before the template can be used.
It prepares the template request with review constraints in mind.

best_practices: Search Rube tools first so the assistant uses current schemas.
Confirm recipient consent, phone number format, and message window status before sending.
Use approved templates for business-initiated messages outside the 24-hour window.
anti_patterns: Do not send bulk outreach without consent and approved templates.
Do not paste sensitive customer data unless the Rube and WhatsApp data flow is approved.
Do not use expired media IDs or private media URLs for customer messages.

faq.question: Does this work with personal WhatsApp accounts?
faq.answer: No. The skill is written for WhatsApp Business API accounts connected through Rube MCP.

faq.question: Why does the skill search tools before each workflow?
faq.answer: Rube tool schemas can change. Searching first helps the assistant use the current fields and tool names.

faq.question: Can it send messages outside the 24-hour customer window?
faq.answer: Yes, but only through approved WhatsApp template messages that match the required language and variables.

faq.question: Can it upload and send media files?
faq.answer: Yes. It documents media upload, media ID sending, and public URL sending with WhatsApp format limits.

faq.question: What data should users review before using it?
faq.answer: Review message bodies, recipient numbers, contacts, media, and business profile data before sending through external services.

faq.question: Is the skill blocked for security reasons?
faq.answer: No. The audit found no malicious code, but networked WhatsApp automation requires privacy and compliance review.
